rulings.Case Title: CAMILLE MOORE VS. PETER ROSSI WALL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 17, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT PETER WALL, 504 MANAGEMENT COMPANY LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants¿ motion for summary judgment is denied in its entirety. There are triable issues regarding what caused the fire, whether defendant had or should have had notice of the electrical defects, whether defendant knew of the danger and concealed it and/or made false representations regarding the safety of the premises, and whether defendant acted with a conscious disregard towards the safety of the tenants. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IRA SERVICES INC. VS. AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 17, 2011, Line 2, PLAINTIFF IRA SERVICES INC.'s Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena - Plaintiff¿s motion to quash subpoena is denied. This motion should be heard by a New York court. Even if this court is the proper forum to hear the motion, the motion would be denied due to lack of merit based on the defendant¿s representation that it is not seeking any information of individual customers. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER KO et al VS. PRAETORIAN NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 17, 2011, line 19. DEFENDANT PRAETORIAN INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants¿ demurrer to complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to defendant York Claims Service and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to defendant Praetorian Insurance Company. York is not a proper party to this case since it is not a party to the insurance contract. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to explain why their calculations differ from Praetorian¿s and why plaintiffs¿ calculations are supported by the policy language; otherwise there is no dispute that requires a declaration. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)
